summary: The R34 is the most refined GT-R of the RB26 era. It shares the same engine strengths and age-related weaknesses as the R32/R33, but commands significantly higher prices. Maintain meticulously.

issues.name: Ceramic Turbo Wheels
issues.description: The factory T28 turbos with ceramic exhaust wheels can shatter with age. Given the R34's value, upgrading to ball-bearing steel-wheel turbos is strongly recommended.

issues.name: Getrag V160 Transmission Weakness
issues.description: The 6-speed Getrag V160 can suffer synchro wear, especially on modified cars. Replacement transmissions are rare and extremely expensive.

issues.name: Multi-Function Display Failure
issues.description: The R34's iconic center console multi-function display (MFD) can fail. Replacement units are rare. Aftermarket solutions exist but lose the original aesthetic.

issues.name: ATTESA and HICAS Systems
issues.description: Both the AWD and four-wheel steering systems use aging electronics and hydraulics that require maintenance and can develop faults.

issues.name: Rust and Corrosion
issues.description: As a 20+ year old JDM car, rust is a concern. The R34's extreme value makes any rust particularly problematic for resale.

buyingTips: The R34 GT-R commands six-figure prices — any purchase should include a professional pre-purchase inspection
Verify authenticity meticulously — the R34's extreme value creates incentive for VIN fraud and chassis swaps
The RB26DETT engine is the same legendary unit from the R32/R33 — same strengths, same age-related concerns
Check the Getrag V160 transmission for smooth operation — replacements cost as much as some entire cars
Factor in the cost of proper storage and maintenance — an R34 GT-R is an investment-grade vehicle that requires care
